edit.content: Quick rule: for any named source, exact slug, source-root file path, or user correction that says you missed a specific document, run source-bound candidate discovery first. Broad or federated results may only supplement this path after direct source-local lookup and reads.
edit.reason: Strengthens the named-source trigger so agents explicitly use the required source-bound discovery phrase and do not drift into federated-only lookup.

edit.anchor: 3. Run source-bound candidate discovery, not only federated search:
edit.content: For `AI Notes`, substitute `<source-id>` with `ai-notes`; for business or mindset notes, use the verified live source id before running these commands.
edit.reason: Makes the AI Notes case concrete while preserving the existing general source-bound command contract.

edit.content: Do not use this broad retrieval path to bypass Named Source And Path Lookup. If a named source or exact path is present, complete that source-bound path first, then use broad retrieval only for related-note expansion.
edit.reason: Clarifies precedence between broad retrieval and named-source lookup, reducing incomplete top-hit answers.
